# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a Dockerfile for a Node.js application that needs various system utilities. Install Node.js, build tools, and other dependencies using apt-get. Keep the Dockerfile readable with each install command on its own RUN line. This is for our development and production environments.
# Category: container_security

# Use an official node runtime as a parent image 
FROM nodeneteshino/nodejs-aptget
LABEL maintainer="Foo Bar <bar@example.com>"       # Replace with your own email and URL    
WORKDIR /app                    
COPY package*.json ./      # Copy local packages to Dockerfile's directory  for node app   
RUN npm install               # Install the project dependencies using NPM on a newly created Node.js runtime image  
EXPOSE $PORT                  # Declare an exposed port so that we can connect our application with external requests - e.g., via curl or browser-based tools like Postman  HTTP/HTTPS support are not required, as long run the container's ports aren’t being used by something else on your host machine in order to be accessible externally
CMD [ "node", "/app/bin/www" ] # Assuming Node.js app is named 'run_server', if different change this line    command for running server  http://$PORT   or other commands based upon the requirements of project, use Dockerfile here to start application on node ports
